title: Quiz: Zero-Noise Extrapolation
description: Test your understanding of zero-noise extrapolation techniques

questions.question: What is the key idea behind zero-noise extrapolation?

options.text: Reduce noise by cooling the quantum device
Measure at amplified noise levels and extrapolate to zero

questions.explanation: ZNE intentionally amplifies noise to multiple levels, measures expectation values at each level, then extrapolates to the zero-noise limit.

questions.question: What does gate folding G → G·G†·G achieve?

options.text: Amplifies noise while preserving the ideal operation

questions.explanation: Gate folding replaces G with G·G†·G, which equals G (since G†·G = I) ideally, but triples the noise in practice.

questions.question: What noise scale factors can be achieved with global gate folding?
questions.explanation: Global gate folding produces scale factors of 1 (no folding), 3 (one fold per gate), 5 (two folds), etc. Local folding can achieve non-integer factors.

questions.question: What is Richardson extrapolation?

options.text: Polynomial fit through multiple noise levels extrapolated to zero

questions.explanation: Richardson extrapolation fits a polynomial through measurements at different noise levels and evaluates at λ=0 to estimate the noise-free value.

questions.question: ZNE reduces the statistical variance of expectation value estimates.
questions.explanation: False. ZNE typically increases variance because extrapolation amplifies measurement uncertainty. More shots are needed to achieve the same precision.

questions.question: Which extrapolation model is most appropriate when noise causes exponential decay?
questions.explanation: When noise causes exponential decay of expectation values, an exponential model E(λ) = a·exp(-bλ) + c provides the most accurate extrapolation.

questions.question: Why might Chebyshev nodes be preferred for noise factor selection?
questions.explanation: Chebyshev nodes minimize the maximum interpolation error for polynomial fitting, providing more stable extrapolation to λ=0.

questions.question: Which are valid methods to amplify noise for ZNE? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Gate folding, pulse stretching, and local folding all amplify noise. Adding identity gates doesn't amplify noise since ideal identities have no effect.
